figures.caption: Fig. 1 Concept of sliding-ferroelectricity driven spin-layertronic in an altermagnetic bilayer. Upon switching of the FE states (FE-I and FE-II), the built-in electric polarization is reversed. The reversal of the FE polarization causes the altermagnetic spin-split bands to energetically shift in the opposite direction for the two sub-monolayers, thus leading to altermagnetic spin switching.
Fig. 2 Energy profiles of bilayer CuF2 along (a) x- and (b) y-directions. (c) Crystal structures of FE-I, IM, and FE-II states from side and top views.

Fig. 3 Layer-resolved spin-polarized band structures of (a) FE-I and (b) FE-II states with the contribution from the top layer. Inset in (a) is the isoenergy contours near the Fermi level of FE-I state. Layer-resolved spin-polarized band structures of (c) FE-I and (d) FE-II states with the contribution from the bottom layer. Inset in (d) is the isoenergy contours near the Fermi level of FE-II state. Density of states of (e) FE-I and (f) FE-II states. Fermi level is set to zero in (a) to (f). Longitudinal and transverse spin conductivities in (g) FE-I and (h)FE-II states. Insets are schematic diagrams of the layer-polarized spin currents in FE-I and FE-II states.

Fig. 4 Layer-resolved spin-polarized band structures and schematic diagrams of layerpolarized spin current in (a) (+2,+1,+2), (b) (+1,−1,−1), (c) (−2,−1,−2), and (d) (−1,+1,+1) states. Fermi level is set to zero.
